description: Production-derived intent question (frontend audit 2026-07-07): a correctness reviewer blocked on 'the Lock/Unlock button state is inverted' when the truth lives in the design spec, not the code - the author's 'the implementation matches the design' cannot be refuted or confirmed from the repo. The validator must return plausible, so the claim posts as a non-blocking suggestion (never a block) and the PR approves with one comment.

evidence_trace: src/classroom/lock-toggle.tsx:24 renders the Lock action with lockIcon when isLocked is true
an already-locked classroom arguably should offer Unlock
finding.failure_scenario: With `isLocked` true the button reads `Lock`, so a teacher clicking an already-locked classroom issues another lock instead of the intended unlock.
finding.model_authored_prose: The Lock/Unlock button looks inverted: when `isLocked` is true it renders `Lock` with `lockIcon`, but an already-locked classroom should offer `Unlock`. If the design intends a state label rather than an action label, ignore this.
